behavior_summary: U8P_EVILSORC::cast is the sorcerer's spell-casting entry: it prepares process metadata (set info / process exclude), forwards a 2-byte spell descriptor plus caster, caller pid and target, and spawns a SPELCMBT combat/spell handler to execute the spell. After spawning it propagates the spawn return value (implies), performs cleanup and suspends.

U8P_EVILSORC::look handles examination: it queries Npc::isDead(self) and barks either a dead or alive short description ("dead Sorcerer " vs "Sorcerer ") to the requesting pid, then cleans up and suspends.

U8P_EVILSORC::use handles player interaction/theft: if the NPC is alive and not already in combat it becomes aggressive toward the activator by calling Npc::setTarget. On a specific map (map id 0x0E) it first emits a threatening bark and suspends briefly (likely a dramatic pause) before setting the target.
notable_control_flow: U8P_EVILSORC::cast: set process info / exclude -> push spell descriptor (2 bytes), push caster (self), push pid, push target -> spawn SPELCMBT::084D -> propagate retval via implies -> pop temp -> suspend.
U8P_EVILSORC::look: set process info / exclude -> call Npc::isDead(self) -> choose between two static bark strings -> call Item::bark -> free string -> implies/pop -> suspend.

U8P_EVILSORC::use: set process info / exclude -> if Npc::isDead(self) == 0 and Npc::isInCombat(self) == 0 then: if Npc::getMap(self) == 0x0E then bark threatening line to pid and suspend; afterwards (or if not map 0x0E) call Npc::setTarget(self, 0x01) to become aggressive.
open_questions: Exact binary encoding and semantic meaning of the 2-byte spell descriptor forwarded by U8P_EVILSORC::cast (which bits indicate spell id, power, or target behavior) — appears as a packed descriptor but format is not present in this script.

Precise text of the theft-threat bark in U8P_EVILSORC::use (only inferred as a threatening line), and whether the suspend after barking has gameplay timing effects beyond flavor (e.g., interrupts player actions).

Whether any global 'info id' values set by the 'set info' primitive (used across handlers) have wider stateful effects for this NPC type (the script uses set info / process exclude but the exact keys/meanings are not present here).
Whether the 'target' parameter passed to SPELCMBT is always a player id or can be other NPC/process ids (target type inferred but not guaranteed).

main_function.name: U8P_EVILSORC::cast
main_function.purpose: Accept a spell-U8P_EVILSORC::cast request from this NPC, prepare process metadata and exclusion, then spawn the spell-combat handler (SPELCMBT) with the spell descriptor, caster, caller pid and target so the spell effect is resolved by a separate combat process.
entry_conditions: Event 11 (U8P_EVILSORC::cast) — invoked when the sorcerer attempts to U8P_EVILSORC::cast a spell (external trigger).

function_map.name: U8P_EVILSORC::cast
function_map.role: Spawn and dispatch a spell-combat process using a forwarded 2-byte spell descriptor, caster (self), pid and target.

function_map.name: U8P_EVILSORC::look
function_map.role: Describe/examine the NPC: query life state (Npc::isDead) and bark the appropriate short description to the viewer pid.

function_map.name: U8P_EVILSORC::use
function_map.role: React to player 'use' action: if alive and not in combat, optionally bark (map-dependent) and set the NPC's combat target to the activator (make the NPC aggressive).

function_map.name: Npc::isDead
function_map.role: Query whether the NPC instance is dead; used to gate look and use behaviors.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Emit/display a short text message/dialogue from this NPC/item to a target pid; used by look and use to communicate messages.

function_map.name: Npc::isInCombat
function_map.role: Check whether this NPC is currently engaged in combat; prevents redundant aggression.

function_map.name: Npc::getMap
function_map.role: Return current map id for the NPC; used to trigger map-specific behavior (map 0x0E special-case).

function_map.name: Npc::setTarget
function_map.role: Set the NPC's combat target (effectively making it aggressive toward the given id).

function_map.name: SPELCMBT::084D
function_map.role: Spawn/entry point for the spell-combat process which executes the actual spell effect using the passed descriptor, caster, pid and target.

function_map.name: U8P_EVILSORC::usecode primitives (set info / process exclude /spawn /implies /suspend /free string)
function_map.role: Engine-level primitives used to set process metadata, prevent interference, spawn subprocesses, propagate return values, pause execution, and manage temporary strings.

external_calls.purpose: Spawn the separate spell-combat process that resolves the U8P_EVILSORC::cast using the passed spell descriptor, caster, pid and target.
Decide whether to bark the dead or alive short description.
Emit the short examination string to the requesting pid (either "dead Sorcerer " or "Sorcerer ").
Ensure the NPC is alive before reacting aggressively to being used/stealed from.
Avoid setting a new target if the NPC is already in combat.
Check for map-specific behavior (map id 0x0E) to decide whether to bark and suspend before engaging.
Emit a threatening line to the activator on map 0x0E prior to engagement.
Set the NPC's combat target to the activator/player (make NPC hostile).

external_calls.callee: U8P_EVILSORC::usecode primitives (set info / process exclude /implies /suspend /free string)
external_calls.purpose: Manage process metadata, prevent interference from other processes, propagate spawn return values and suspend execution for timing/control.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_EVILSORC::cast is the sorcerer's spell-casting entry: it prepares process metadata (set info / process exclude), forwards a 2-byte spell descriptor plus caster, caller pid and target, and spawns a SPELCMBT combat/spell handler to execute the spell. After spawning it propagates the spawn return value (implies), performs cleanup and suspends.
